comment.body: Well, the thing is, if you shorted volatility on Thursday, as I've said many times, in order to be in alignment with her analysis spot would have needed to continue to decline. However, as I stated, she actually ended up being correct on shorting volatility even though spot ripped by 200 points. So for her to say on Thursday "if market rips, that's spot up/vol up + low liquidity" and then to take credit for shorting volatility even though it went contradictory to her own analysis, I think that deserves criticism. This kind of instance is what criticism is for. Moreover, she did not take responsibility or account for volatility acting in the exact opposite way of what she said it would on Thursday. If she did, and transparently explained why the opposite happened, then I think your response here would be a lot more warranted. But instead, she has acted like she has called it flawlessly.

comment.body:
I think so many folks on this chat overthink things.  Alma has repeatedly shared that it's a supportive OPEX.  The detail she gets into on her intraday posts are for those who want the detail of an intraday move, like very fast options trades.  

Plus if you just use her SPX script each morning for the Mean Reversion Thresholds/Momentum Measure you would have seen we bounced right off the BOTTOM RISK LEVEL this morning.  

Don't overthink it y'all, especially if you're looking for a trade beyond an hour.

comment.body:
here i covered the collar: https://stochvoltrader.substack.com/p/intraday-post-13march
and I will again, after opex, bcs it is not in effect yet, and it is not magnetic. thats a misinterpretation circulating in the soc media.
if I'm right with the further decline, that will be a vol up spot down kind of decline.
if it will happen before or after the collar expires EOM, I don't know. Post-OpEx, we are back into day by day approach, with a net short bias.
simply bcs insiders WILL push the prices lower. 

Read what I dropped today about tech. It's crucial.
NVDA, AAPL, GOOG are underpefroming. Not a coincidence. Arabians don't buy into tech that has high chinese interest in it. 
they won't give money to Xi. I know it for fact. They bought IBIT now
Wall Street wants and likely will buy China out of them. 
So more down is coming. But without liquidity who will sell? You know...
